title: 10 best herbal creams for wheezing

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal purple coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating wheezing symptoms.

Antihistamine properties: Purple coneflower cream contains antihistamine compounds that can help block the action of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that can cause bronchospasm and wheezing.

Expectorant properties: The herbal extract in purple coneflower creams also has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with wheezing because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating wheezing symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects help to regulate the immune system's response to allergens, reducing the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes. This makes Echinacea a promising natural remedy for wheezing sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Polyphenols (specifically, Chlorogenic acid): As antioxidants, polyphenols can help protect lung cells from oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory function, which may alleviate wheezing.

Triterpene Glycosides (specifically, Alkylamides): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and reduce inflammation in the airways, potentially alleviating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain high levels of alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate wheezing symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are rich in polysaccharides, which stimulate the immune system and help reduce inflammation associated with wheezing.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated taking approximately 30 minutes.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and 5 grams of dried echinacea purpurea leaves with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and plant material mixture at a temperature between 60 to 70 degrees celsius over the next 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and thicken for 1 to 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in licorice creams have antioxidant properties that can help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to wheezing.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice creams have natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and wheezing symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with wheezing because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe irritated airways. The active compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators, reducing congestion and relieving wheezing symptoms. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to calm and protect the mucous membranes, promoting easier breathing and reducing the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes. This makes licorice creams a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, helps alleviate wheezing by reducing inflammation in the airways and exerting anti-asthmatic properties due to its ability to suppress the production of leukotrienes, which contribute to bronchospasm.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin, a flavanone, aids in wheezing relief by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, which are key factors in the pathogenesis of asthma and wheezing.

Licoricidin: Licoricidin, an amyrin glycoside, helps to alleviate wheezing by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the reduction of airway inflammation and the suppression of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thus providing relief from wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to relieve wheezing and coughing.
Barks: They are used due to their mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots, chopped into small pieces, in the morning.
Steam distill the chopped roots with 3 liters of water for 2 hours to obtain the extract.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax, 100 grams of coconut oil, and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Mix 100 grams of the glycyrrhiza extract with 100 grams of distilled water and add to the melted beeswax mixture.
Stir the mixture well and pour into containers, allowing to cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in marshmallow root helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the airways that can contribute to wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Marshmallow herbal creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Soften and lubricate mucus: The mucilage content in marshmallow root also helps to soften and lubricate excess mucus, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which can contribute to wheezing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with wheezing because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the plant contains mucilages, thick, protective compounds that help to calm and protect irritated mucous membranes in the lungs and airways. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the airway muscles, Althaea officinalis creams can help to alleviate wheezing and other respiratory symptoms associated with conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with wheezing by forming a protective layer on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation, and making it easier to breathe.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid helps with wheezing by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Althaea flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol and quercetin): Althaea flavonoids help with wheezing by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce airway inflammation, ease congestion, and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm irritated airways.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain similar properties to the roots, including mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways.
Stems: The stems are used for their mucilaginous properties as well, providing relief from wheezing and coughs by coating and soothing the irritated respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried althaea officinalis root in a cool dark place to preserve its medicinal properties.
Steep 30g of dried althaea officinalis root in 500ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Combine the cooled herbal tea with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as eucalyptol and cineole, have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax bronchial muscles and relieve spasms that contribute to wheezing.

Mucolytic properties: The saponins and terpenoids found in Tasmanian Blue Gum have mucolytic properties, which can help break down excess mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and wheezing symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with wheezing because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The main active compound, eucalyptol, is released when the cream is applied topically, creating a cooling sensation that helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation. This, in turn, relieves wheezing and coughing associated with respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and COPD. By providing quick and localized relief, Eucalyptus globulus creams offers a convenient and effective way to manage wheezing symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol helps with wheezing by acting as a decongestant, relieving congestion in the airways and making it easier to breathe.
Cineole: Cineole, a major component of eucalyptus oil, helps to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to exhale and relieving wheezing symptoms.
β-caryophyllene: β-caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus oil extracted from the leaves is often used in creams for wheezing due to its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Eucalyptus oil from the buds is also used in creams to provide relief from wheezing and congestion due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them completely to prevent mold formation.
Combine 50g of dried leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the leaves from the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20% beeswax and 10% vitamin e oil, then heat the mixture gently until the wax melts.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Thyme's antispasmodic properties can help relax and calm down airway spasms, reducing wheezing and making breathing easier.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to wheezing by narrowing airways.
Expectorant property: Thyme's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with wheezing because of its expectorant properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. This can provide relief from wheezing and other respiratory symptoms associated with conditions like asthma and bronchitis. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, further alleviating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and combat infections in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to wheezing.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the severity of wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of thymol, a compound known for its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Employed for their similar properties to the leaves, including antimicrobial and expectorant actions that help relieve wheezing.
Stems: Utilized for their thymol content, which helps to open airways and reduce congestion associated with wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25 grams of dried thymus vulgaris in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Add 20 milliliters of distilled water to the beeswax and oil mixture while continuously stirring.
Pour the mixture into a tin and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lavender's antihistamine properties help to reduce inflammation and relieve itching, which can exacerbate wheezing symptoms caused by allergic reactions or asthma.

Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: The calming effects of lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for wheezing in asthmatic individuals, leading to reduced frequency and severity of episodes.

Expectorant properties: Lavender's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, a common symptom associated with wheezing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with wheezing because of its potential to relax and calm the airways. The anti-inflammatory properties in lavender oil may help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for wheezing. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and improve airflow, which can alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing caused by allergic reactions or other inflammatory conditions.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the airways from oxidative damage and inflammation, reducing the severity of wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function.
Stems: They are used due to their content of silicic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe respiratory issues such as wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 50g carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for infusion.
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar for a 2-week infusion period.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the oil from solids.
Mix 50g of the strained oil with 20g beeswax and 10g vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Whisk the mixture constantly until it reaches 45°c to 50°c and then pour into containers to cool.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve wheezing symptoms.

Bronchodilatory effects: The herb has been shown to exhibit bronchodilatory properties, which means it can help relax and dilate the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing episodes.

Antioxidant properties: Joe Pye Weed's antioxidant compounds may help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eupatorium perfoliatum, also known as joe pye weed, creams helps with wheezing because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the respiratory system. By reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calm, Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help alleviate wheezing in individuals with respiratory conditions, such as asthma or chronic bronchitis. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help alleviate wheezing by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce airway inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions.

Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Sesquiterpenes found in Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help relieve wheezing by exhibiting bronchodilatory properties, which can relax airway muscles and improve breathing.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and caffeic acid): Phenolic acids present in Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help alleviate wheezing by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eupatorium perfoliatum leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve wheezing and congestion.
Roots: Eupatorium perfoliatum roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and suppress coughs, making them an effective treatment for wheezing.
Flowers: Eupatorium perfoliatum flowers are used due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help relax the airways and relieve wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves from a trusted supplier in the morning.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Mix 20 grams of the essential oil with 80 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure uniform blending.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams contain compounds like allantoin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, thus alleviating wheezing.
Antispasmodic: The plantain extract in these creams can relax smooth muscle spasms in the lungs and airways, reducing bronchial constriction and helping to ease wheezing symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with wheezing because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing congestion and inflammation. The creams' active ingredients, such as aucubin and baicalein, have been shown to relax bronchial muscles, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, plantain creams may help to reduce mucus production and thin out existing mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving wheezing symptoms. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Plantago major may help alleviate wheezing by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways.

Triterpenoids (specifically aucubin): Aucubin, a triterpenoid found in Plantago major, may exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help ease wheezing by loosening and removing mucus from the airways.

Phenolic acids (specifically ferulic acid): Ferulic acid, a phenolic acid found in Plantago major, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, thereby alleviating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Plantago major are used to make creams for wheezing due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues.
Seeds: The seeds of Plantago major are used to make creams for wheezing because they contain mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried plantago major leaves to use in your herbal cream recipe.
Infuse the dried leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as grapeseed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil from the dried leaves and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm the airways, reducing wheezing and coughing by easing bronchial spasms.

Expectorant properties: Fennel cream's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs. This leads to improved breathing and a reduction in wheezing symptoms. Fennel creams may also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and further alleviating wheezing. remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: This sesquiterpene is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
Anethole: A phenolic compound found in Foeniculum vulgare, anethole has bronchodilatory effects, allowing airways to dilate and reduce wheezing.

Leaves: The leaves are used for their rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and volatile oils, which can help relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems are used for their mucilaginous properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a warm oven at 50c for 2 hours to preserve the essential oils.
Combine 200g of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100g of sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 60c to infuse the oil with foeniculum vulgare essence.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and whip 100g of beeswax with the oil mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Angelica's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in angelica cream can help reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to wheezing.
Bronchodilator properties: Angelica's bronchodilatory effects can help relax and open up constricted airways, making it easier for air to flow through and reducing wheezing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica, also known as angelica, creams helps with wheezing because of its anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties. The herb contains compounds like angelic acid and isoangelic acid, which have been shown to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, angelica's expectorant properties help to clear mucus from the airways, further alleviating wheezing symptoms. remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help to relax the airways and reduce wheezing.

Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, leading to relief from wheezing.

Cariofilin: This phenolic glycoside has been found to possess bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from wheezing.

Leaves: They contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects to relieve wheezing.
Buds: They are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh angelica roots in late summer or early fall when plant tops have dried.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried angelica roots with 2 cups of olive oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture for 4 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit then strain the liquid using cheesecloth.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal infusion with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with wheezing because of its natural ability to relax the airway muscles, allowing for easier breathing. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect on the throat and lungs, which helps to reduce inflammation and open up congested airways. Additionally, the herbal properties in peppermint cream have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which further help to ease wheezing by reducing swelling and constriction in the airways. As a result, peppermint creams can provide quick relief from wheezing and make breathing easier.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, helps to thin mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and wheezing caused by respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and other breathing difficulties.
Expectorant action: The expectorant properties of peppermint oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing caused by excess phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain powerful analgesic and antispasmodic properties that can ease congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of fresh peppermint leaves dried or one teaspoon of dried peppermint powder.
Combine the peppermint leaves or powder with four ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes allowing the menthol and other compounds to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and let it cool.
Add three tablespoons of beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the cooled peppermint oil mixture whipping until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with wheezing because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The creams contain compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the lungs. Additionally, dandelion's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as triterpenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and polyphenols, help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to wheezing.
Expectorant effects: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm respiratory issues like wheezing.
Buds: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves in early morning dew for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 70-80°f for 3-4 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of powdered taraxacum officinale with 10ml of coconut oil and 5ml of beeswax, heating the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool until solidified, then strain through cheesecloth to create a smooth, creamy texture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with wheezing because they contain a potent combination of natural ingredients that soothe and calm inflamed airways. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, relieving wheezing symptoms by opening up air passages.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of yarrow cream help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing the severity of wheezing attacks and promoting easier breathing.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow's antispasmodic compounds relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing constriction and spasms that can exacerbate wheezing, allowing for deeper and more efficient breathing.

Seeds: Containing expectorant compounds, seeds of yarrow stimulate mucus secretion and aid in removing excess phlegm from the lungs, relieving wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest acmillefolium flowers in mid-summer and dry them thoroughly for 7-10 days.
Grind dried yarrow flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Melt 100g of beeswax in a double boiler at 180°f for 15 minutes while gently stirring.
Combine the ground yarrow powder with 50g of coconut oil and stir until well blended for 2 minutes.
Slowly add the melted beeswax to the yarrow-coconut oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The herb's essential oils, such as thujone and camphor, have a calming effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and constriction of air passages. This relief from congestion and swelling allows for easier breathing and reduced wheezing sounds, providing natural and gentle relief to individuals suffering from respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate respiratory issues like wheezing, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salvene, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, soothe bronchial tubes, reduce swelling, and ease congestion caused by wheezing, making it easier to breathe.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier in bulk weighing about 100 grams.
Combine the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and steep for four hours under gentle heat at room temperature.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the liquid from the solids discarding any remaining plant material.
Mix one teaspoon of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil and gently warm over low heat until melted then stir well to combine.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars sealing tightly and store in an airtight container for up to six months refrigerating before use if needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop creams helps with wheezing because they contain the essential oils of the hyssop plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for the body to expel mucus and other irritants that can cause wheezing. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects of hyssop creams can also help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and other respiratory issues.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds present in hyssop, including eucalyptol and borneol, help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving wheezing symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: Hyssop's antispasmodic compounds, such as alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, help relax the bronchial muscles and reduce spasms, which can contribute to wheezing and other respiratory problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including wheezing, due to their antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: The seeds of hyssop contain a high amount of essential oils that help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring quality and freshness for optimal potency.
Combine the dried herb with 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar, steeping it for 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil with its herbal properties.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 ounces of distilled water in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the wax melts and emulsifies smoothly.
Remove the cream from heat, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Winterflower creams helps with wheezing because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated airways. The herbal extracts, such as Echinacea and Marshmallow, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier for individuals to breathe. Barks: The bark contains antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and promote healthy breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bergenia crassifolia leaves and stems, carefully rinsing them with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one part dried bergenia crassifolia leaves with two parts coconut oil in a glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 6-8 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20% of the strained oil with 80% beeswax in a double boiler, heating until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat, letting it cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized jars and allowing it to solidify completely at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with wheezing because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway constriction, thereby alleviating wheezing symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source in late summer or early fall.
Dry the plant material using air circulation at room temperature for seven to ten days or using a food dehydrator on low setting.
Grind 10 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric spice grinder.
Combine 5 grams of powdered verbena officinalis with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for twenty-five minutes.
Stir the mixture every five minutes until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f, then remove from heat and let cool before transferring to glass jars.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them gently with cold water.
Dry the leaves in a dehydrator at 30°c for 3 hours or air-dry them for 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100ml of coconut oil and heat them at 60°c for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting oil infusion with 10% beeswax and whip it until it forms a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work together to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating constriction and making it easier for air to flow through the lungs. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to wheezing symptoms.

Bronchodilatory properties: The saponins present in fenugreek cream have a bronchodilating effect, which can help relax airway smooth muscle, reducing constriction and opening up airways to improve breathing.
Mucolytic properties: Fenugreek cream's mucolytic properties can break down and dissolve excess mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and wheezing symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried fenugreek leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with the ground fenugreek powder in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 5 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to infuse for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool completely before transferring the mixture to a clean glass jar, which will require 30 minutes.
Store the herbal cream in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4 degrees celsius and use within 6 months or until expiration date.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted supplier or your own garden in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or by air-drying them for 3-4 days.
Combine 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 14 days of infusion to produce a strong herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 200ml of cooled down beeswax and 100ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler while gently stirring until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for wheezing are topical treatments that help alleviate respiratory issues by soothing and calming the airways. They typically contain ingredients like eucalyptus, menthol, and peppermint, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. Examples of herbal creams that help with wheezing include those containing thyme and eucalyptus, which have been shown to improve breathing and reduce wheezing symptoms. This can greatly improve the quality of life for individuals suffering from wheezing, enabling them to engage in daily activities with greater ease and comfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to wheezing that are treated with herbal creams are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). These conditions involve inflammation and constriction of the airways, leading to coughing, shortness of breath, and wheezing. Herbal creams containing ingredients like eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger have natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help alleviate these symptoms by soothing the respiratory tract and loosening mucus.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with wheezing is a blend of Peppermint, Eucalyptus, and Thyme creams. Peppermint helps to open airways and calm the respiratory system, while Eucalyptus oil reduces inflammation and congestion. Thyme, rich in antiseptic properties, helps to combat infections and promote healing. Applying these creams topically, particularly on the chest, can provide relief from wheezing. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal creams, especially if you have underlying health conditions or allergies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, the study involved a review of literature using the PubMed and OVID databases, searching for keywords asthma, allergy, and complementary and alternative medicine (CAM) from 1980 to 2003. The study focused on 12 herbs, including Echinacea, garlic, angelica, chamomile, ephedra, ginkgo, grape seed extract, licorice root, St. John's wort, kava kava, peppermint oil and leaf, and stinging nettle, to identify their effects on asthma and allergy conditions. The results found that while most of these herbs have adverse effects, such as gastrointestinal disturbances, allergic reactions, and hepatotoxicity, Echinacea is commonly used to treat upper respiratory tract allergies and may be beneficial for wheezing due to its potential to alleviate symptoms of the common cold, which often accompanies asthma and wheezing.
